failure_surface: Code execution failed repeatedly due to syntax errors caused by improperly formatted tool call markers embedded in the executable Python code
root_cause: The agent submitted code containing markdown-style tool invocation markers (e.g., '\n\n<function=code_interpreter>', '{content=') directly as executable Python, causing SyntaxError. The execution environment could not parse these non-Python characters as valid syntax.
minimal_fix_hypothesis: Before submitting code for execution, ensure the code block contains only valid, clean Python without tool invocation wrappers or markdown formatting. Extract the inner content properly before sending to the interpreter.
evidence: Cell In[1], line 1 showing 'SyntaxError: invalid syntax' with caret pointing to '<tool_call>'
Cell In[1], line 5 showing 'SyntaxError: unterminated string literal (detected at line 5)' pointing to '{content='
Multiple consecutive execution attempts all failing with identical syntax errors
The same underlying logic was submitted 4+ times but never parsed successfully

memory_items.title: Clean Code Submission
memory_items.lesson: Ensure code blocks sent to execution environments contain only valid language syntax without embedding tool markers, markdown headers, or JSON wrapper strings directly in the executable content.
memory_items.generalization: When using a multi-step tool chain where code generation feeds into an interpreter, strip intermediate formatting artifacts before submission.

memory_items.title: Syntax Validation Before Execution
memory_items.lesson: Implement pre-execution checks or validate code structure programmatically before attempting runtime execution to catch formatting issues early.
memory_items.generalization: A lightweight syntax check on generated code can prevent wasted execution cycles on obviously malformed submissions.

memory_items.title: Tool Response Parsing Discipline
memory_items.lesson: Extract the intended payload from tool responses carefully, distinguishing between metadata/headers and actual executable content.
memory_items.generalization: Never treat a tool call envelope as part of the function argument; always isolate the inner content before passing to downstream processors.

edits.content:
## Code Execution Hygiene

### Clean Submission Protocol
- Ensure all code sent to interpreters contains only valid syntax in the target language.
- Strip tool invocation markers, markdown fences, and JSON wrapper structures before execution.
- Never embed intermediate formatting artifacts (like function-call envelopes) within executable payloads.

### Pre-Execution Validation
- Run a lightweight syntax check on generated code before attempting runtime execution.
- If syntax errors occur on first attempt, re-examine the raw submission string for hidden markers or truncated content.
- Distinguish between tool response metadata and actual working content when constructing downstream requests.

### Multi-Step Tool Chain Awareness
- In chained workflows where code generation feeds an interpreter, maintain clear boundaries between each tool's responsibility.
- Document which portions of output are for routing vs. which are for direct execution.
- Implement sanity-check loops that detect obvious formatting failures before consuming compute resources.
